Table Tennis England case study provides direct, relatable proof. Estimated CPL improvement vs current AXIS campaign based on tighter persona-message fit.

name: VP Fan Engagement & Digital Experience — Professional Sports Teams (Top 5 Leagues)
angle: Fan retention and time-spent: JORDY AI's 30% M1 retention boost and 140% time-spent increase are metrics this persona is directly accountable for. Current JORDY AI campaign targets Sports Teams broadly — this narrows to the VP/Director-level digital buyer at clubs in top-tier leagues.
why: JORDY AI campaign is already the best performer (CTR 0.81%, CPL $90.38) but targets Sports Teams broadly. Narrowing to the VP Digital/Fan Experience persona at clubs in top-5 global leagues (Premier League, NBA, NFL, LaLiga, Bundesliga tier) should improve CPL further by increasing message-persona fit. This buyer owns app engagement, second-screen experience, and fan retention metrics — exactly what JORDY AI moves. The 140% time-spent and 30% M1 retention numbers are board-level KPIs for these clubs.
geo: UK, USA, Spain, Germany, Australia
headline: Fans open the app. Then leave. Here's the fix.
body:
The average sports fan spends 4 minutes in a club app before churning.

Clubs using Jordy AI — Spectatr's fan engagement agent — see 30% better first-month retention and 140% more time spent per session.

Jordy serves personalised highlights, real-time moments, and interactive content — automatically, for every fan, at scale.

60% of users discover content they'd never have found manually.

→ See Jordy in action.
reason: Builds on JORDY AI's strongest-performing campaign (CTR 0.81%, CPL $90.38) with tighter persona targeting. Narrower audience + stronger message-fit = expected CPL reduction below $90. High ACV accounts (top-tier clubs) justify premium CPL even if it rises. Untested at this segmentation level.

name: Multi-Sport Federation Digital Directors — Global
angle: Scale proof — ISG/NSL case studies
why: Multi-sport federations (Olympic committees, continental games, regional associations) have the highest clip volume requirements and the largest potential PULSE contract values. ISG Riyadh 2025 (50,000+ clips, 57 NOCs) is a direct peer-reference for this audience. Currently untested as a standalone LinkedIn segment — likely bundled into a broader 'Leagues' audience, which dilutes relevance and CPL efficiency.
geo: Global (prioritise: Middle East, Europe, Asia-Pacific)
headline: 50,000 AI clips. 57 countries. 15 days. Is your federation next?
body:
At the Islamic Solidarity Games Riyadh 2025, PULSE generated 50,000+ real-time highlights for 57 National Olympic Committees — 512 published daily, 7.5 million views generated.

No editors. No delays. Every moment, every nation, in seconds.

If you’re managing a multi-sport event in 2026 or beyond, this is the benchmark for AI-powered content at scale.

👉 See the full ISG case study.
reason: Multi-sport federations are the highest-value PULSE buyers (event-scale contracts, multi-year deals). ISG proof point creates instant credibility. Estimated CPL on LinkedIn for this segment: $80–150 (high intent, niche audience). Segment is untested and should be isolated from the broader Leagues audience for clean CPL benchmarking.

name: OTT Platform Content & Technology Leaders — Europe & APAC
angle: Broadcast reliability + volume — PULSE as infrastructure
why: OTT platforms (regional sports streamers, telco sports products, rights-holder OTT) are evaluating AI highlights infrastructure for 2026–2027 rights cycles. Their buyer is the VP of Content or CTO, and their objection is reliability at scale, not feature lists. This is a distinct segment from 'Broadcasters' and should be tested separately. No evidence this segment is currently targeted.
geo: Europe (UK, DACH, Nordics), Asia-Pacific (Australia, SE Asia)
headline: Real-time AI highlights, built for OTT scale. No editors. No delays.
body:
OTT audiences expect match highlights within seconds of the moment — not minutes, not hours.

PULSE delivers: AI-generated clips, published automatically, across 15+ sports.

At the Islamic Solidarity Games: 512 highlights/day. 50,000+ clips. 15 days. Zero manual intervention.

Built for the volume your platform demands.

👉 Book a 20-minute technical demo.
reason: OTT rights cycles are accelerating in Europe and APAC. AI highlights infrastructure decisions are being made now for 2026–2027. 'Book a Demo' CTA suits a technical evaluator buyer (CTO/VP) better than a content download. Segment isolation from general 'Broadcasters' allows CPL benchmarking by buyer type.

name: Sports Club CMOs & Heads of Digital — Professional Teams (UK, Australia, MENA)
angle: Fan retention + content ROI — JORDY AI + PULSE bundle
why: Professional sports clubs (football, rugby, basketball, cricket) have a clear digital KPI stack: M1 retention, time-in-app, content engagement. JORDY AI’s 30% M1 retention and 140% time-spent metrics map directly to this KPI stack. Simultaneously, PULSE’s real-time clips solve the content volume problem that drives poor retention. A bundled JORDY+PULSE message for club CMOs is currently untested and addresses two pain points in one narrative.
geo: United Kingdom, Australia, UAE, Saudi Arabia
headline: 30% more fans stayed after Month 1. Here’s what changed.
body:
Most sports clubs publish great content.

Most fans still churn within 30 days.

The problem isn’t the content — it’s the distribution. Fans aren’t seeing the moments that matter to them.

Jordy AI fixes that: serving the right content to the right fan, automatically.

Result: 30% M1 retention boost. 140% increase in time spent. +60% new content discovered.

Same content. Smarter delivery.

👉 See how Jordy works for clubs like yours.
reason: UK, Australia, and MENA are Spectatr’s strongest existing case study markets (HockeyOne = Australia, NSL = Australia, ISG = MENA). Geographic relevance increases ad resonance and CTR. Club CMOs in these markets are actively investing in fan engagement tech for 2026 season. Bundle angle (JORDY + PULSE) creates cross-sell opportunity from a single lead.
